population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: USP35 asterisked = colocalized eQTL in sun-exposed lower leg skin tissue AND independently carries its own coding (nonsynonymous) lead variant rs2511188 (p.Ile677Val, P=7.42e-7 for L*, below genome-wide significance but reported separately in Supplementary Data 5) AND is in the 12-of-36 highest-melanocyte-expression gene set. Strongest multi-evidence non-canonical candidate in this paper. GAB2 (nearest-gene label) is ALSO in the highest-melanocyte-expression set and carries the disease-association citation (with GLIS1, SEM1) for inflammatory epidermal conditions/melanoma -- both genes have real evidence; USP35 favored for the coding+eQTL combination.

population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: BCO1 (beta-carotene oxygenase 1) not colocalized, not canonical, no explicit mechanism discussed in main text beyond general fibroblast-expression clustering. mygene.info confirms BCO1 is 'a key enzyme in beta-carotene metabolism to vitamin A' -- biologically plausible for a carotenoid/yellowness (b*) phenotype by analogy to SCARB1 (idx20, carotenoid-uptake gene) given both act in the carotenoid pathway, but this specific analogy is not stated by the paper's authors -- flagged as an independently-corroborated inference, not an author claim.

classification_notes: SLC6A17 asterisked = colocalized eQTL in sun-exposed lower leg skin; in the 12-of-36 highest-melanocyte-expression set; named in Discussion strengths paragraph as having functional evidence in post-GWAS analyses; and the paper cites prior literature that 'a genetic variant of the membrane transporter gene SLC6A17 was associated with pigmentation-related traits (tanning ability)'. population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: SNORC is the asterisked/colocalized gene at this locus (eQTL evidence), but the Discussion gives an explicit BIOLOGICAL mechanism for UGT1A6 instead: 'known to induce yellowish iris pigmentation...genetically associated with...Gilbert's syndrome (yellowish discoloration of the skin)...involved in the conjugation of vitamin D' -- also named in the strengths paragraph as having functional evidence in post-GWAS analyses. Two candidate genes with two different, non-overlapping lines of evidence (UGT1A6=biological/disease mechanism, SNORC=eQTL) -- genuinely uncertain which is causal; UGT1A6 favored given the specificity of its cited mechanism. Neither gene canonical.

population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: RAB32 not canonical, but Discussion explicitly states a demonstrated mechanism: 'KITLG, RAB32, and SPIRE2 in melanosome transport or dispersion within melanocytes' (citing refs 35,36) -- and RAB32 is in the 12-of-36 highest-melanocyte-expression set. mygene.info independently confirms: 'appears to be involved in autophagy and melanosome secretion' -- strong convergent non-canonical candidate with an explicit, specific melanosome-trafficking mechanism from TWO independent sources (paper's Discussion + mygene.info). Zhang2018 melanocyte eQTL cross-check: RAB32 tested in curated379 gene set but did not reach eGene significance in melanocytes in that dataset (blank q-value) -- Zhang2018's melanocyte-eQTL analysis does not corroborate RAB32 as a melanocyte cis-eQTL target, though this does not contradict its melanosome-trafficking protein function cited by Kim2024's Discussion and mygene.info.

population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: TRPS1 not canonical. Discussion states 'TRPS1 and KITLG in the proliferation of epithelial cells and melanocytes' (citing refs 32,33) as a functionally-associated melanin-synthesis-pathway gene -- BUT a separate Discussion sentence describes TRPS1 as one of the 'genes that have not been investigated for their functional role in pigmentation' and only 'associated with tanning ability and skin cancer'. This is an internal tension in the source paper's own text (flagged verbatim, not resolved by this extraction) between a stated melanocyte-proliferation mechanism and a caveat that pigmentation-specific function is uninvestigated.

population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: SCARB1 not canonical, but Discussion explicitly states a demonstrated mechanism: 'SCARB1 influences skin yellowness by promoting the uptake of carotenoids' (citing refs 29,30). mygene.info confirms SCARB1 is 'a plasma membrane receptor for high density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL)' mediating 'cholesterol transfer to and from HDL' -- consistent with (though not identical to) the paper's carotenoid-uptake claim; SCARB1 is well-documented in the literature as also mediating carotenoid uptake via the same receptor pathway.

population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: 16q24.3 locus. Nearest-gene label is SPIRE2 (not canonical); all three genes (SPIRE2, DEF8, CPNE7) asterisked = colocalized eQTL in sun-exposed lower leg skin. CRITICAL near-gene case: this locus is ~70kb from the separate MC1R lead variant (idx24) at the same cytogenetic band, but the paper explicitly distinguishes the two signals and states 'the eQTLs of the colocalized genes...were in LD with the lead variant in SPIRE2' -- i.e., the regulatory evidence points to SPIRE2/DEF8/CPNE7 specifically, NOT to MC1R, despite MC1R's well-known function and proximity. Classified as effector_uncertain (demonstrated regulatory evidence for non-canonical genes), NOT regulatory_of_canonical_neighbour, because the authors explicitly reject the MC1R attribution for this particular variant. SPIRE2 also appears in the melanosome-transport mechanism sentence ('KITLG, RAB32, and SPIRE2 in melanosome transport or dispersion') giving it independent corroborating evidence beyond eQTL colocalization.

population: East Asian (South Korean discovery cohort, N=48,433; replication N=4,992)
classification_notes: MFSD12 NOT on the task's canonical list (consistent with legacy-paper treatment in Ang2023 idx39 and Crawford2017 idx43). Missense variant; MFSD12 is in the 12-of-36 highest-melanocyte-expression set; Discussion explicitly states: 'MFSD12 contributes to red-yellow pigmentation by maintaining cysteine levels within melanosomes' and further '(3) OCA2 and MFSD12 in the regulation of amino acid intake into melanosomes'. This is now a THIRD independent paper (Ang2023, Crawford2017 [CRISPR-validated], Kim2024) converging on MFSD12 as a demonstrated, non-canonical effector.
